name: Ascent to Alto de la Veiga and Miners
name_original: Asensción al Alto de la Veiga — circular desde Astorga por la senda de los Mineros
description: This difficult 37.7-mile mountain biking route from Astorga, known as Senda de los Mineros, climbs 3441 feet to Alto de la Veiga.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Astorga are found in a region characterized by its transition from the expansive Páramo Leonés plain to the rugged Montes de León mountain range. This geographical diversity provides a variety of terrains, from gentle slopes to challenging ascents and descents. The area is situated in the northwestern part of the Duero Basin, featuring a series of ridges and the fertile plain of the Tuerto River. The surrounding Maragatería region also contributes picturesque landscapes and viewpoints. Best mountain bike trails around Astorga The most popular mountain bike trail is Ascent to Alto de la Veiga and Miners, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a significant climb to Alto de la Veiga. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Adif Astorga – Plaza Mayor Building Facades loop from Astorga, a difficult 62.8 miles (101.0 km) path. This expansive loop connects urban areas with the surrounding rural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Gaudí Palace – Plaza Mayor Building Facades loop from Astorga, a 31.7 miles (51.0 km) trail leading through historic landmarks and surrounding countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Astorga is defined by varied terrains, from the plains to the Montes de León, and routes along the Tuerto River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult climbs. The routes in Astorga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.95 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 370 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Astorga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Astorga?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Astorga listed on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Astorga?
A: The region around Astorga offers varied terrain, transitioning from the expansive Páramo Leonés plain to the rugged Montes de León mountain range. You'll find everything from gentle slopes to challenging ascents and descents, with routes along the Tuerto River and through the picturesque Maragatería region.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Astorga?
A: Yes, Astorga offers several options for less experienced riders. There are 7 easy mountain bike trails available, providing a good starting point to explore the area's natural beauty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19881948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaudí Palace – Plaza Mayor Building Facades loop from Astorga&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that can be a good introduction.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Astorga?
A: While mountain biking is possible year-round, the popular cycling months are often March and May. These months typically offer pleasant weather conditions for exploring the trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes in Astorga?
A: Yes, Astorga is home to several challenging routes. For experienced riders, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032316027/ascent-to-alto-de-la-veiga-and-miners" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Alto de la Veiga and Miners&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 37.7-mile (60.7 km) trail with a significant climb. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adif Astorga – Plaza Mayor Building Facades loop from Astorga&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 62.8 miles (101.0 km).

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Astorga?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Astorga are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19881948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaudí Palace – Plaza Mayor Building Facades loop from Astorga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17541958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Ganso Village – Plaza Mayor Building Facades loop from Astorga&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular routes that allow you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Astorga's trails often pass by significant historical and natural sites. You can encounter remnants of its Roman past, including well-preserved Roman walls. The region is also part of the historic Camino de Santiago. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5898729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Saint Mary of Astorga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4637297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Episcopal Palace of Astorga (Gaudí Palace)&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby Sierra de Teleno is known for its wildlife.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Astorga?
A: While specific family-friendly trails are not explicitly categorized, many of the 7 easy and 32 moderate trails could be suitable for families, depending on their experience level. Look for routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances for a more relaxed family outing.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Astorga?
A: The mountain bike trails in Astorga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.95 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, which offers a mix of plains and mountain landscapes, and the opportunity to explore historic landmarks and picturesque villages.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that follow parts of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, Astorga is a significant stop on the Camino de Santiago, and sections of this historic pilgrimage route are popular for mountain biking. These routes offer a unique blend of cultural heritage and outdoor adventure.

Q: Where can I find viewpoints with panoramic vistas along the mountain bike routes?
A: The surrounding Maragatería region, which many trails traverse, is known for its picturesque landscapes and numerous viewpoints that offer panoramic vistas. Keep an eye out for elevated sections of trails, especially those climbing into the Montes de León, for the best views.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the traditional Maragatería villages?
A: Yes, several routes will take you through or near traditional Maragatería villages such as Castrillo de los Polvazares, Santa Colomba de Somoza, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabanal del Camino&lt;/a&gt;. These villages offer a cultural immersion alongside your ride, showcasing ancient trades and distinctive architecture.
tips: Rolling along the path of the Miners, we come across this monument that remembers those who risked their lives to go to work in the mines of Santibañez de Montes and Montealegre between 1900 and 1960.

Rolling through pine forests and countless paths to choose from, we arrive at Alto de la Veiga where magnificent views of the Montes de León await us. For sure, during our route we will cross small villages (some uninhabited) hidden among pine trees that will surprise us.
Rolling along the wide path of Foncebadon, under the imposing gaze of the large mills, we come across this beautiful image next to the crosses known as the miners'.

faqs.question: What level of fitness and experience is required for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and requires very good physical fitness and considerable mountain biking experience. Riders should be prepared for technical sections and the possibility of needing to push their bikes in certain parts.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Senda de los Mineros?
faqs.answer: The terrain is challenging and rugged, consistent with its 'difficult' rating. While specific ground cover details are not extensively provided, the name 'senda de los Mineros' (miners' path) suggests varied and potentially technical surfaces, possibly including sections where pushing your bike is necessary.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or historical features along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting points. You'll encounter the Miners' Monument, reach the Alto de la Veiga (1564 m) summit, and pass the Miners' Crosses of Foncebadón. The trail itself follows historical mining routes, adding a unique cultural dimension.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located in the province of León, Castilla y León, a region known for its diverse landscapes, you can expect scenic views throughout this demanding ride. The ascent to Alto de la Veiga will offer panoramic vistas of the surrounding mountains and countryside.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Asensción al Alto de la Veiga mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Astorga. While specific parking lots are not detailed, you would typically find parking within Astorga itself to begin this circular trail. Look for public parking areas in the town center or on its outskirts.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog regulations for this particular trail is not available. However, when mountain biking with dogs in Spain, it's generally advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to ensure they are fit enough for such a long and challenging ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this challenging route would generally be during the spring or autumn months. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, which are ideal for a long and strenuous ride. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring snow or icy conditions at higher elevations like Alto de la Veiga.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a specific permit or entrance fee is required to mountain bike the Asensción al Alto de la Veiga — circular desde Astorga por la senda de los Mineros trail. It is generally open to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de Tras de la Cuesta, Camino Foncebadon, Camino del Sierro, Calle Fondovilla, and Camino de Fonfría.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this mountain biking adventure?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and difficulty, you should pack essentials such as plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate protective gear (helmet, gloves), navigation tools (like the komoot app), and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs along the route or near the trailhead in Astorga?
faqs.answer: While specific establishments along the trail are not detailed, Astorga, as the starting and ending point, offers various cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's advisable to carry sufficient provisions for the duration of the trail itself.
